title: Crash on wire declaration with delay for declaration assignment.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

The `read_verilog` command crashes on parsing the following MCVE:

```
module testcase;
    wire [3:0] #1 a = 4'b0000;
endmodule
```

This syntax is covered in the language specification as follows:

```
net_declaration ::= ...
     | net_type [ drive_strength ] [ vectored | scalared ] [ signed ] range [ delay3 ] list_of_net_decl_assignments;

list_of_net_decl_assignments ::=
    net_decl_assignment { , net_decl_assignment }

net_decl_assignment ::= 
    net_identifier = expression
```

## Expected behavior
Correct assignment of the constant to the wire, discarding the delay specifier.

## Actual behavior
```
# yosys testcase.v
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: testcase.v
Parsing Verilog input from `testcase.v' to AST representation.
testcase.v:2: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected '#', expecting TOK_ID
```
code_blocks:
# yosys testcase.v
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: testcase.v
Parsing Verilog input from `testcase.v' to AST representation.
testcase.v:2: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected '#', expecting TOK_ID
